title: An RIA is stripping out software from $1 billion-plus of acquisitions -- with a new $48.5 million raise and sky-high praise -- but critics say Arca Wealth mostly has a new narrative for an existing business model
summary_raw: The New York City startup unveiled itself yesterday as an all-in, 'AI-native,' wunderkind that is in the process of 'removing' applications and shedding its old Schwab custody and robo-investing ties -- and gets ex-Schwab CFO Peter Crawford as backer.

summary_ai.digest: Arca Wealth, an NYC-based RIA, closed a $48.5M Series B raise and is repositioning as an AI-native platform while divesting software from $1B+ in prior acquisitions and moving away from Schwab custody relationships. Ex-Schwab CFO Peter Crawford is backing the round.
summary_ai.highlight: $48.5M Series B for Arca Wealth; strategic custody migration away from Schwab and software consolidation signal potential M&A or platform rationalization play in RIA consolidation space.
